OPPOSITION TO SYNERGY PROJECT MANAGEMENT, INC. OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE PRESENTED TO PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES (TRANSACTION ID # 57536185) FILED BY DEFENDANT PACIFIC GAS & ELECTRIC COMPANY

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT PACIFIC GAS & ELECTRIC COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION - MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. AS TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF THE 1974 SWAP AGREEMENT, PLAINTIFF FAILS TO PRESENT ANY EVIDENCE THAT SUCH A WRITTEN AGREEMENT EXISTS. PLAINTIFF RELIES ON THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S ("CCSF"). HOWEVER, CCSF'S RESPONSES STATE THAT THE AGREEMENT IS ATTACHED TO ITS FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT AS EXHIBIT 5. THAT EXHIBIT IS A CONTRACT BETWEEN CCSF AND PACIFIC TELEPHONE AND TELEGRAPH COMPANY, NOT PG&E. WITHOUT ANY EVIDENCE THAT A WRITTEN AGREEMENT EXISTS, PLAINTIFF CANNOT MAINTAIN ITS CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF A WRITTEN CONTRACT. AS TO THE QUANTUM MERUIT CAUSE OF ACTION, THE EVIDENCE ESTABLISHES THAT EXPRESS CONTRACTS EXISTS DEFINING THE PARTIES' RIGHTS, AND THEREFORE, THE COURT CANNOT, AS A MATTER OF LAW, "SUBSTITUTE THE COURT'S OWN CONCEPTS OF FAIRNESS REGARDING THE SUBJECT IN PLACE OF THE PARTIES' OWN CONTRACT." AS TO THE COMMON COUNTS CAUSE OF ACTION, BECAUSE THE OTHER CAUSES OF ACTION FAIL, PLAINTIFF CANNOT MAINTAIN A COMMON COUNTS ACTION THAT IS WHOLLY DERIVATIVE. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE ARE OVERRULED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES - MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. THE UNDISPUTED EVIDENCE ESTABLISHES THAT PLAINTIFF ENTERED INTO VALID, BROAD RELEASES THAT WAIVED ALL CLAIMS AGAINST DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ("CCSF") ARISING UNDER THE CONTRACTS. THE FACTS HERE ARE DISTINGUISHABLE FROM THOSE IN THE CASES CITED BY PLAINTIFF IN OPPOSITION, AS PLAINTIFF WAS AWARE THAT IT WAS NOT PAID FOR THE SWAP COSTS, AND WAS THEREFORE AWARE OF ITS INJURY, AT THE TIME THAT IT EXECUTED THE GENERAL RELEASES. THE COURT SHOULD LOOK TO THE CONTRACT LANGUAGE AND DETERMINE IF THE RELEASE LANGUAGE IS UNAMBIGUOUS. HERE, THE LANGUAGE IN THE RELEASES IS UNAMBIGUOUS AND PROVIDES FOR A RELEASE OF ALL CLAIMS PLAINTIFF MIGHT HAVE AGAINST CCSF. ADDITIONALLY, THE UNDISPUTED EVIDENCE ESTABLISHES THAT PLAINTIFF DID NOT FILE A TIMELY GOVERNMENT CLAIM. GOV. CODE SEC. 911.2(A) PROVIDES THAT A BREACH OF CONTRACT CLAIM MUST BE PRESENTED ONE YEAR AFTER THE ACCRUAL OF THE CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF'S CAUSE OF ACTION ACCRUED, AT THE VERY LATEST, AT THE TIME THE WORK WAS COMPLETED AND PLAINTIFF WAS AWARE THAT IT WAS NOT PAID FOR THE SWAP COSTS. PLAINTIFF COMPLETED ITS WORK UNDER THE CONTRACTS IN JUNE 2010, BUT DID NOT PRESENT ITS GOVERNMENT CODE CLAIM UNTIL JANUARY 2014. (UMF 89, 94.) FURTHER, THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS BARS PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS. GOV. CODE SEC. 945.6(A)(2) PROVIDES THAT A BREACH OF CONTRACT CAUSE OF ACTION MUST BE BROUGHT AGAINST A PUBLIC ENTITY WITHIN TWO YEARS OF ACCRUAL OF THE CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF COMPLETED ITS WORK IN JUNE 2010, BUT DID NOT FILE SUIT UNTIL FEBRUARY 2014. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. Defendant Pacific Gas & Electric Company'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jul-24-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. KRIS COX OF THE MOSCONE EMBLIDGE & OTIS LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-362-3599 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. LAURIE EDELSTEIN OF THE STEPTOE LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 1-650-687-9570 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT PG&E.

RULING - A R G U E D; Defendant Pacific Gas & Electric Company's Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication - Motion for summary judgment is granted. As to the cause of action for breach of the 1974 SWAP Agreement, Plaintiff fails to present any evidence that such a written agreement exists. Plaintiff relies on the City and County of San Francisco's ("CCSF"). However, CCSF's responses state that the agreement is attached to its First Amended Cross-Complaint as Exhibit 5. That exhibit is a contract between CCSF and Pacific Telephone and Telegraph Company, not PG&E. Without any evidence that a written agreement exists, Plaintiff cannot maintain its cause of action for breach of a written contract. As to the quantum meruit cause of action, the evidence establishes that express contracts exists defining the parties' rights, and therefore, the court cannot, as a matter of law, "substitute the court's own concepts of fairness regarding the subject in place of the parties' own contract." As to the common counts cause of action, because the other causes of action fail, Plaintiff cannot maintain a common counts action that is wholly derivative. Plaintiff's objections to evidence are overruled. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Suzanne Bolanos; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/sb

Defendant City And County Of San Francisco'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication Of Issues
Off Calendar Jul-27-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. KRIS COX OF THE MOSCONE EMBLIDGE & OTIS LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-362-3599 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JOSEPH SANDOVAL, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 1-415-554-3867 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO;
ATTY. DAVID R. HOBSTETTER, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 1-415-554-3981 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; Defendant City And County Of San Francisco's Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication Of Issues - Motion for summary judgment is granted. The undisputed evidence establishes that Plaintiff entered into valid, broad releases that waived all claims against Defendant City and County of San Francisco ("CCSF") arising under the contracts. The facts here are distinguishable from those in the cases cited by Plaintiff in opposition, as Plaintiff was aware that it was not paid for the SWAP costs, and was therefore aware of its injury, at the time that it executed the general releases. The court should look to the contract language and determine if the release language is unambiguous. Here, the language in the releases is unambiguous and provides for a release of all claims Plaintiff might have against CCSF. Additionally, the undisputed evidence establishes that Plaintiff did not file a timely Government Claim. Gov. Code sec. 911.2(a) provides that a breach of contract claim must be presented one year after the accrual of the cause of action. Plaintiff's cause of action accrued, at the very latest, at the time the work was completed and Plaintiff was aware that it was not paid for the SWAP costs. Plaintiff completed its work under the contracts in June 2010, but did not present its Government Code claim until January 2014. (UMF 89, 94.) Further, the statute of limitations bars Plaintiff's claims. Gov. Code sec. 945.6(a)(2) provides that a breach of contract cause of action must be brought against a public entity within two years of accrual of the cause of action. Plaintiff completed its work in June 2010, but did not file suit until February 2014. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Suzanne Bolanos; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-05-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Judge Miller disclosed the hearing that she is a member of McFetridge Inn of Court and that Laurie Edelstein, counsel for defendant/cross-defendant PG&E is also a member of the same Inn of Court. This will not affect Judge Miller's ability to be fair and impartial in this case and she does not believe that a person aware of this fact would reasonably entertain a doubt that she would be able to be impartial. No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS-DEFENDANT PACIFIC GAS & ELECTRIC COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. (CCP sec. 430.10(e).) As to the 1st cause of action, the plain language of the Franchise Agreements, attached as exhibits to the Cross-Complaint, require the utility company to "remove or relocate" facilities as necessary to accommodate construction projects. (Cross-Complaint, Ex. A, p. A-5, Ex. B, p. B-3.) There is no contractual obligation that the utility company pay for any work around or support of their facilities. Therefore, the Cross-Complaint does not adequately allege breach. As to the 2nd cause of action, the 1974 SWAP Agreement, attached as an exhibit to the Cross-Complaint, explicitly states that it is between "the Pacific Telephone and Telegraph Company'and the City and County of San Francisco." (Cross-Complaint, Ex. E, p. E-1.) As such, the Cross-Complaint does not adequately plead that PG&E is a party to the Agreement. The 1970 SWAP Agreement, attached as an exhibit to the Cross-Complaint, does not contain a provision requiring the utility company to make payment directly to a private contractor. (Cross-Complaint, Ex. D.) Therefore, the Cross-Complaint does not adequately allege breach. As to the 3rd cause of action, the Cross-Complaint does not allege facts establishing that Sections 906-910 were violated. As to the fifth cause of action, the 1970 SWAP Agreement provides that the utility company must indemnify the City as to claims "arising directly or indirectly from defects in Contract Utility Drawings incorporated in any City contract." (Cross-Complaint, Ex. D, p. D-12.) The Cross-Complaint does not allege damages from defects in Contract Utility Drawings. As to the 7th cause of action, the Cross-Complaint alleges no facts establishing that Cross-Defendant has an obligation to pay Synergy directly under any contract or statute. Therefore, facts are not sufficiently pled to support a declaratory relief cause of action. Cross-Complainant's request for judicial notice is granted. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR # 11185, Tel. No. (951)764-4047 =302/mjm

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-05-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Judge Miller disclosed at the hearing that she is a member of McFetridge Inn of Court and that Laurie Edelstein, counsel for defendant/cross-defendant PG&E is also a member of the same Inn of Court. This will not affect Judge Miller's ability to be fair and impartial in this case and she does not believe that a person aware of this fact would reasonably entertain a doubt that she would be able to be impartial. No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT PACIFIC BELL TELEPHONE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is Overruled in part and sustained without leave to amend in part. As to the quantum meruit cause of action, the demurrer is overruled. Liberally construing the Second Amended Complaint ("SAC"), the SAC adequately alleges an implied request and benefit. (SAC, pars. 70-71.) As to the account stated cause of action, the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. The SAC does not plead a previous relationship between the parties. (Zinn v. Fred R. Bright Co. (1969) 271 Cal.App.2d 597, 600; see also 1 Witkin, Summary 10th (2005) Contacts, sec. 972, p. 1062, stating that an account stated is where "[p]arties transacting business with each other, and keeping accounts of their transaction and items of indebtedness'come to an agreement on the amount of the final balance due from one another.")
